# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

// Allowlist of permitted modules that can be dynamically loaded
const ALLOWED_MODULES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
  "./plugins/logger.ts",
  "./plugins/analytics.ts",
  "./plugins/transformer.ts",
  "./plugins/validator.ts",
  "./plugins/formatter.ts",
]);

// Base directory for plugins - prevents directory traversal
const PLUGIN_BASE_DIR = "./plugins/";

// Interface that all plugins must implement
interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  execute(input: unknown): Promise<unknown>;
}

function isValidPlugin(mod: unknown): mod is { default: Plugin } {
  if (typeof mod !== "object" || mod === null) return false;
  const m = mod as Record<string, unknown>;
  if (typeof m.default !== "object" || m.default === null) return false;
  const plugin = m.default as Record<string, unknown>;
  return (
    typeof plugin.name === "string" &&
    typeof plugin.version === "string" &&
    typeof plugin.execute === "function"
  );
}

function sanitizeModulePath(rawPath: string): string | null {
  // Trim whitespace
  const trimmed = rawPath.trim();

  if (!trimmed) return null;

  // Block any protocol-based URLs (prevents loading remote/arbitrary modules)
  if (/^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9+\-.]*:\/\//.test(trimmed)) {
    return null;
  }

  // Block data: URIs
  if (trimmed.toLowerCase().startsWith("data:")) {
    return null;
  }

  // Normalize the path to prevent directory traversal
  // Reject paths containing "..", "~", or absolute paths
  if (
    trimmed.includes("..") ||
    trimmed.includes("~") ||
    trimmed.startsWith("/") ||
    trimmed.startsWith("\\")
  ) {
    return null;
  }

  // Ensure the path starts with the plugin base directory
  let normalizedPath = trimmed;
  if (!normalizedPath.startsWith(PLUGIN_BASE_DIR)) {
    normalizedPath = PLUGIN_BASE_DIR + normalizedPath;
  }

  // Ensure it ends with .ts (only TypeScript modules allowed)
  if (!normalizedPath.endsWith(".ts")) {
    return null;
  }

  // Final check: must be in the allowlist
  if (!ALLOWED_MODULES.has(normalizedPath)) {
    return null;
  }

  return normalizedPath;
}

// Cache loaded plugins to avoid repeated dynamic imports
const pluginCache = new Map<string, Plugin>();

async function loadPlugin(modulePath: string): Promise<Plugin> {
  // Check cache first
  const cached = pluginCache.get(modulePath);
  if (cached) return cached;

  const sanitized = sanitizeModulePath(modulePath);
  if (!sanitized) {
    throw new Error(
      `Module path "${modulePath}" is not allowed. Only allowlisted plugin paths are permitted.`
    );
  }

  try {
    const mod = await import(sanitized);

    if (!isValidPlugin(mod)) {
      throw new Error(
        `Module "${sanitized}" does not export a valid Plugin interface.`
      );
    }

    const plugin = mod.default;
    pluginCache.set(modulePath, plugin);
    return plugin;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof Error && error.message.includes("not allowed")) {
      throw error;
    }
    throw new Error(
      `Failed to load plugin "${sanitized}": ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`
    );
  }
}

// Load plugins specified via environment variables at startup
async function loadConfiguredPlugins(): Promise<Map<string, Plugin>> {
  const plugins = new Map<string, Plugin>();

  // Read comma-separated plugin paths from env var
  const envPlugins = Deno.env.get("PLUGINS") || "";
  if (envPlugins) {
    const paths = envPlugins.split(",").map((p) => p.trim()).filter(Boolean);
    for (const path of paths) {
      try {
        const plugin = await loadPlugin(path);
        plugins.set(plugin.name, plugin);
        console.log(`Loaded plugin from env: ${plugin.name} v${plugin.version}`);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(
          `Failed to load env plugin "${path}": ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : err}`
        );
      }
    }
  }

  return plugins;
}

// Rate limiting: simple in-memory rate limiter per IP
const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetTime: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 30;

function isRateLimited(ip: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(ip);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetTime) {
    rateLimitMap.set(ip, { count: 1, resetTime: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return false;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return entry.count > RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS;
}

// Periodically clean up rate limit entries
setInterval(() => {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [ip, entry] of rateLimitMap) {
    if (now > entry.resetTime) {
      rateLimitMap.delete(ip);
    }
  }
}, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS);

const startupPlugins = await loadConfiguredPlugins();

serve(
  async (req: Request, connInfo?: { remoteAddr?: { hostname?: string } }): Promise<Response> => {
    const clientIp =
      connInfo?.remoteAddr?.hostname ||
      req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
      "unknown";

    if (isRateLimited(clientIp)) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later." }),
        {
          status: 429,
          headers: {
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "Retry-After": "60",
          },
        }
      );
    }

    const url = new URL(req.url);

    // Health check endpoint
    if (url.pathname === "/health") {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ status: "ok", loadedPlugins: [...startupPlugins.keys()] }),
        {
          status: 200,
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // List available plugins
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins" && req.method === "GET") {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          allowedModules: [...ALLOWED_MODULES],
          loadedPlugins: [...startupPlugins.entries()].map(([name, p]) => ({
            name,
            version: p.version,
          })),
          cachedPlugins: [...pluginCache.keys()],
        }),
        {
          status: 200,
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // Load and execute a plugin
    if (url.pathname === "/execute" && req.method === "POST") {
      const pluginPath = url.searchParams.get("plugin");

      if (!pluginPath) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: "Missing 'plugin' query parameter.",
            allowedModules: [...ALLOWED_MODULES],
          }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      // Validate the path before attempting to load
      const sanitized = sanitizeModulePath(pluginPath);
      if (!sanitized) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: `Plugin path "${pluginPath}" is not permitted. Only allowlisted modules can be loaded.`,
            allowedModules: [...ALLOWED_MODULES],
          }),
          {
            status: 403,
            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      let input: unknown = null;
      try {
        const contentType = req.headers.get("content-type") || "";
        if (contentType.includes("application/json")) {
          const body = await req.text();
          // Limit body size (prevent DoS via large payloads)
          if (body.length > 1_048_576) {
            return new Response(
              JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body too large. Max 1MB." }),
              {
                status: 413,
                headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
              }
            );
          }
          input = JSON.parse(body);
        }
      } catch {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid JSON in request body." }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      try {
        const plugin = await loadPlugin(pluginPath);

        // Execute with a timeout to prevent long-running plugins
        const timeoutMs = 5000;
        const result = await Promise.race([
          plugin.execute(input),
          new Promise((_, reject) =>
            setTimeout(
              () => reject(new Error("Plugin execution timed out")),
              timeoutMs
            )
          ),
        ]);

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            plugin: { name: plugin.name, version: plugin.version },
            result,
          }),
          {
            status: 200,
            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      } catch (err) {
        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
        const status = message.includes("not allowed") || message.includes("not permitted")
          ? 403
          : 500;

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
          {
            status,
            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }
    }

    // Default: not found
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: "Not found",
        availableEndpoints: {
          "GET /health": "Health check",
          "GET /plugins": "List available and loaded plugins",
          "POST /execute?plugin=<path>": "Execute a plugin with JSON body as input",
        },
      }),
      {
        status: 404,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  },
  { port: 8000 }
);

console.log("Plugin server running on http://localhost:8000");
console.log(`Startup plugins loaded: ${[...startupPlugins.keys()].join(", ") || "none"}`);
console.log(`Allowed modules: ${[...ALLOWED_MODULES].join(", ")}`);
